data.title: Bitmine has staked another 113,300 ETH, bringing its total staked amount to approximately 2,266,000 ETH.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Bitmine (@BitMNR) has staked an additional 113,280 ETH, worth approximately $341 million. As of now, Bitmine's total staked ETH has increased to 2,265,984, valued at approximately $6.82 billion at current prices.

data.title: Ethereum to Launch ERC-8004 Standard on Mainnet
data.description: Ethereum has officially announced the upcoming launch of the ERC-8004 standard on its mainnet. According to ChainCatcher, this new standard will enable AI agents to interact across various organizations by implementing a discovery mechanism and portable reputation. This development aims to facilitate the flow of reputation globally, paving the way for an interoperable market for AI services.

data.title: Steak 'n Shake: Bitcoin holdings increased in notional value by $5 million
data.description: Steak ‘n Shake announced on the X platform that its transformation from burger to Bitcoin continues. Today, the company's Bitcoin holdings increased by $5 million in notional value. All Bitcoin sales revenue goes into its Strategic Bitcoin Reserve (SBR). The company's self-sustaining system, which drives same-store sales growth by improving food quality and thus increasing its Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, is transforming the chain business through financial technology. Steak ‘n Shake is a subsidiary of Biglari Holdings.

data.title: CZ's "buy and hold" view has been questioned by the community, which argues that it does not apply to all crypto assets.
data.description: CZ posted yesterday that few trading strategies can outperform a simple "buy and hold" strategy, encouraging users to maintain a long-term holding strategy during the crypto bear market. However, this view subsequently sparked controversy within the community. Community user @WazzCrypto cited relevant data pointing out that "if you had bought all the cryptocurrencies listed on Binance contracts last year, your overall portfolio would be close to zero," questioning the universality of the "buy and hold" strategy in the crypto market. In response, CZ stated this morning that this strategy obviously does not apply to all cryptocurrencies. He said it's similar to "buying all internet or artificial intelligence projects/companies," where most projects ultimately fail in any industry, and only a few succeed in achieving exponential growth.

data.title: Hackers suspected of involvement in a $90 million cryptocurrency theft from the US government have transferred over $330 million in ETH to Tornado Cash.
data.description: According to PyShield's monitoring, John (Lick), the hacker suspected of involvement in the 2024 US government's theft of approximately $90 million in crypto assets, has begun transferring related funds to the Tornado Cash mixing protocol. Monitoring data shows that 11,037 ETH have already been transferred to Tornado Cash, equivalent to approximately $330 million at current prices. PyShield further stated that this address is also linked to a series of thefts targeting unidentified victims between November and December 2025, and the flow of these funds is still being tracked.

data.title: The crypto market saw a slight increase, with BTC approaching $90,000, while only the GameFi and DePin sectors experienced a weak decline.
data.description: On January 28th, according to SoSoValue data, the overall crypto market showed a slight upward trend, with the DeFi sector performing particularly well, rising 3.68% in the past 24 hours. Hyperliquid (HYPE) saw a significant increase of 27.77%, Lighter (LIT) rose 11.16%, and Jupiter (JUP) climbed 6.47%. Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 0.82%, approaching the $90,000 mark. Ethereum (ETH) rose 2.67%, breaking through $3,000. Notably, MAG7.ssi rose 1.56%, DEFI.ssi rose 6.67%, and MEME.ssi rose 3.05%. Other sectors that performed well include: AI (up 2.19% in 24 hours, with Kite (KITE) up 7.99%); Meme (up 2.01%, with PIPPIN (PIPPIN) surging 62.31%); and Layer 1 (up 1.83%, with Zcash (ZEC) up 7.38%). In other sectors, CeFi (up 1.52%, with Aster (ASTER) up 3.89%); Layer 2 (up 0.56%, with Mantle (MNT) up 3.28%); RWA (up 0.48%, with Keeta (KTA) up 15.31%); and PayFi (up 0.01%, with Bitcoin Cash (BCH) up 3.12%). Conversely, DePIN and GameFi sectors fell by 0.86% and 1.91%, respectively. The crypto sector indices, which reflect the historical performance of the sector, show that the ssiDeFi, ssiAI, and ssiLayer1 indices rose by 6.19%, 4.38%, and 2.24%, respectively.

data.title: Amazon Shifts Focus to Delivery Services and Whole Foods Expansion
data.description: Amazon has decided to close its Amazon Fresh and Amazon Go physical stores to prioritize its delivery services and expand Whole Foods locations. According to NS3.AI, the company is also planning significant layoffs, impacting approximately 30,000 jobs, and has settled a $1 billion customer refund claim. Despite these changes, Amazon's stock has increased by 2% and has risen 6% in 2026. Analysts remain largely optimistic, with an average price target suggesting over 25% potential growth from the current stock price.

data.title: Wintermute: Bitcoin has been fluctuating for about 60 days, and continued outflows from ETFs indicate that selling pressure is dominant in the US.
data.description: According to Wintermute analysis, Bitcoin's price has been consolidating between $85,000 and $94,000 for about 60 days, with selling pressure in the US market becoming a key factor determining its short-term direction. While Bitcoin briefly rose to $97,000 in early January, it failed to gain sustained buying support and subsequently fell back to the middle of the range. Wintermute points out that market momentum is highly correlated with ETF fund flows: the January rally was accompanied by net inflows into ETFs, while the recent pullback corresponds to record outflows from BTC and ETH ETFs. Simultaneously, the Coinbase premium has turned negative, indicating that US counterparties are generally net sellers, and net inflows of institutional funds through ETFs and corporate treasuries have turned negative, putting pressure on prices. The analysis suggests that for a successful breakout from the current range, it's crucial to monitor whether ETF fund flows and the Coinbase premium simultaneously turn positive. Until then, close attention should be paid to the support level around $85,000. This week's intensive macroeconomic events, including the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and Powell's speech, technology company earnings reports, and progress in tariff negotiations, may become potential catalysts for breaking the deadlock.

data.title: Rick Rieder's Market-Centric Approach Anticipated to Influence Federal Reserve Policies
data.description:
On January 28, Rick Rieder, a seasoned Wall Street professional, is expected to bring a market-focused approach to the Federal Reserve. According to Odaily, last September, Rieder advocated for a more aggressive 50 basis point rate cut, contrasting with the Federal Reserve's preference for a gradual 25 basis point reduction. He also opposed the central bank's forward guidance on future interest rate trends, known as the 'dot plot.'

Economists from Evercore ISI, including Krishna Guha, noted earlier this week that Rieder is likely to adopt a dovish stance on interest rates and may propose three rate cuts this year.

Currently, the interest rate swap market is pricing in less than two 25 basis point cuts by 2026. However, the SOFR options market has recently seen a surge in positions that would benefit from multiple rate cuts, aiming to lower the federal funds rate to as low as 1.5% by the end of the year, significantly below the current interest rate swap market pricing of approximately 3.2%.

data.title: Gate.com and TradFi trading volumes surpass $10 billion; gold hits a new all-time high.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate TradFi, international gold prices have reached a new all-time high, hitting a 24-hour high of $5225.14 per ounce, a cumulative increase of over $910 since the beginning of 2026, representing a rise of over 20%. Meanwhile, since its public beta launch, Gate TradFi's cumulative trading volume has exceeded $10 billion, with accelerated release of demand for multi-asset trading and a steady increase in user participation and activity. Gate TradFi allows users to directly trade traditional financial market products within the platform, providing one-stop coverage of precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. It not only covers a wider range of asset classes, with precious metals, indices, forex, and commodities supporting up to 500x fixed leverage, but also offers more competitive overall transaction fees. Gate TradFi's functions are fully integrated into the Gate App, allowing users to conveniently participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities beyond the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: Bitcoin's network hashrate suffered its largest drop in history, possibly due to extreme weather in the United States.
data.description: According to Cloverpool data, the Bitcoin network hashrate recently plummeted from approximately 1.16 ZH/s to 690 EH/s, marking the largest single drop in its history. The hashrate subsequently rebounded and is currently at approximately 859.17 EH/s. Analysts believe this significant fluctuation may be related to extreme weather in the United States. The winter storm "Fernan" triggered extreme low temperatures, heavy snowfall, and freezing conditions across many parts of the US, causing power outages for over one million residents and prompting several power grid operators to issue energy-saving warnings. Against this backdrop, some mining farms in the US either shut down due to power outages or to maintain grid stability, leading to a significant short-term decrease in the Bitcoin network hashrate.

data.title: Euro Falls Below 1.2 Against the Dollar
data.description: The euro has dropped below 1.2 against the U.S. dollar, experiencing a daily decline of 0.35%. According to ChainCatcher, this marks a significant movement in the currency exchange market.

data.title: An address deposited $8.84 million into Hyperliquid and purchased HYPE shares in batches.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created address (0x9D2...1BfF) deposited $8.84 million USDC into Hyperliquid and placed two TWAP orders, planning to buy HYPE within 5 hours.

data.title: Analysis: If Rick Rieder becomes the new Federal Reserve Chairman, he may cut interest rates three times this year.
data.description: On January 28, Rick Rieder, a Wall Street veteran, was seen as a potential market-centric approach to the Federal Reserve. Last September, he advocated for a more aggressive 50-basis-point rate cut, rather than the Fed's then-preferred 25-basis-point gradual pace. He also opposed forward guidance from the central bank on future interest rate movements, the so-called "dot plot." Economists at Evercore ISI, including Krishna Guha, wrote earlier this week, "He will maintain a dovish interest rate stance and may advocate for three rate cuts this year." Currently, the interest rate swap market is only pricing in fewer than two 25-basis-point rate cuts in 2026. However, the SOFR options market has recently seen a surge in positions that would benefit from multiple rate cuts, aiming to lower the federal funds rate to as low as 1.5% by the end of this year, significantly lower than the current pricing of approximately 3.2% in the interest rate swap market. (Jinshi)

data.title: South Korean Court Upholds Tax on Cryptocurrency Prizes
data.description: A South Korean court has confirmed the decision by tax authorities to impose taxes on cryptocurrency prizes awarded during an exchange trading event. According to NS3.AI, the plaintiff argued that these prizes were part of a ranking competition and should be eligible for a tax deduction. However, the court ruled against this claim, stating that the crypto prizes do not qualify for deductible ranking competitions under South Korea's Income Tax Act. This decision provides clarity on the taxation of cryptocurrency prizes in the country.

data.title: Spacecoin and KAIST Collaborate on Decentralized Satellite Internet Project
data.description: Spacecoin has recently held a technology seminar in collaboration with KAIST's School of Electrical Engineering to explore its decentralized satellite internet project and its implementation strategies. According to NS3.AI, the event was attended by notable figures such as Spacecoin founder Taelim Oh, former senior engineers from Samsung, and researchers from KAIST. The seminar aimed to further the Spacecoin vision and advance the development of SpaceNetwork technology.

data.title: Gold Prices Hit Record High Amid Strong Investor Demand
data.description: Spot gold prices have reached an unprecedented level, exceeding $5,200 per ounce. According to NS3.AI, this milestone highlights the precious metal's increasing value. The surge is attributed to robust investor demand and favorable market conditions for gold.

data.title: Arthur Hayes Analyzes Impact of Yen Depreciation on Global Markets
data.description:
Arthur Hayes has released an article titled 'Woomph,' analyzing the recent depreciation of the yen and the decline in Japanese government bond prices, which are causing disruptions in global financial markets. According to Foresight News, Hayes suggests that the Federal Reserve and the U.S. Treasury might be compelled to intervene directly in the yen exchange rate and the Japanese bond market by expanding their balance sheets, thereby injecting new liquidity into the global fiat currency system.

Hayes notes that a stronger yen against the dollar correlates with a decline in Bitcoin prices. He states that he will not increase risk until it is confirmed that the Federal Reserve is printing money to intervene in the yen and Japanese bond markets. If there is a significant increase in foreign currency-denominated assets on the Federal Reserve's balance sheet, it would be an opportune time to increase Bitcoin holdings. Before the yen's volatility, he had closed positions in high-leverage Bitcoin-related assets such as Strategy and Metaplanet. If his assessment proves correct, he plans to re-enter the market.

While awaiting policy clarity, his fund, Maelstrom, continues to increase its holdings in Zcash, maintaining positions in other quality DeFi tokens. Once the Federal Reserve confirms balance sheet expansion intervention, he will consider increasing investments in DeFi assets like ENA, ETHFI, PENDLE, and LDO.

data.title: Spot gold breaks through $5,200 for the first time
data.description: Spot gold hit a new record high, breaking through $5,200 per ounce for the first time, with a cumulative increase of over $880 in the first month of the new year, exceeding 20%. (Golden Ten)
